skill.name: json-validator
skill.description: Validate, format, fix, convert, and analyze JSON data with clear error messages and practical correction guidance.
skill.summary: Validate and repair JSON data for APIs, configuration files, and structured data workflows.
skill.author: Alon2904

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed locations are Markdown prose or fenced JSON/YAML examples. No executable code, shell command construction, cryptographic implementation, reconnaissance logic, network calls, environment access, or prompt injection text was found. The skill is safe to publish with the static findings dismissed as false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and example JSON/YAML blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations contain documentation examples only; no executable command invocation or command construction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible Markdown fences, example output, or structured data examples. The file contains no script body, shell syntax, or instruction to execute user-controlled commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Text Misclassified as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts point to frontmatter, prose, and JSON examples. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, operating system reconnaissance, filesystem enumeration, network access, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are ordinary skill description text or examples of invalid JSON comments. Semantic review found no code path that performs cryptographic operations or system discovery.

content.value_statement: Broken JSON slows API work, configuration reviews, and data handoffs. This skill validates, formats, repairs, converts, and explains JSON issues in clear steps.
actual_capabilities: Validates JSON syntax and reports clear error locations.
Formats minified JSON with consistent two-space indentation.
Fixes common issues such as trailing commas, single quotes, comments, and unquoted keys.
Converts JSON to YAML, TOML, CSV, or XML with type preservation guidance.
Analyzes top-level structure, nesting depth, key types, and repeated patterns.
Suggests improvements for inconsistent names, deep nesting, and large arrays.
limitations: It does not execute code or call external validation services.
Large files may need to be split before detailed analysis.
Schema validation requires the user to provide the relevant schema.
Ambiguous repairs may require confirmation before changing data meaning.

use_cases.title: Repair API Payloads
use_cases.description: Find syntax errors in request or response payloads and return a corrected version with explanations.

use_cases.title: Clean Configuration Files
use_cases.description: Format and validate JSON configuration before it is committed or shared with a deployment system.

use_cases.title: Review Data Structure
use_cases.description: Summarize object keys, array sizes, nesting depth, and repeated patterns before data transformation work.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Basic JSON
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this JSON. List each syntax issue with its line number, explain the problem, and provide a corrected version.

prompt_templates.title: Format and Preserve Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this JSON with two-space indentation. Preserve all values and data types. Tell me if you changed anything beyond whitespace.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Common JSON Mistakes
prompt_templates.scenario: Repair invalid JSON from a log, API, or config file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Fix this invalid JSON. Check for trailing commas, comments, single quotes, unquoted keys, and invalid values. Explain each repair.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze and Convert Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced structure review and format conversion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this JSON structure, summarize key types and nesting depth, then convert it to YAML while preserving the original data meaning.

output_examples.input: A configuration file contains a trailing comma and one key written without quotes.
output_examples.output: The skill reports two syntax errors, explains why strict JSON rejects them, and returns a corrected configuration.

output_examples.input: A minified API response needs to be reviewed before debugging.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a readable formatted response and summarizes the object keys, arrays, and nesting depth.

output_examples.input: A team needs a YAML version of a valid JSON settings file.
output_examples.output: The skill converts the structure to YAML and notes any areas where arrays or nested objects need review.

best_practices: Validate JSON before formatting or converting it.
Ask before sorting keys or removing comments from user-provided data.
Preserve numbers, booleans, null values, and strings exactly unless the user requests a change.
anti_patterns: Do not silently change data values while fixing syntax.
Do not assume comments are safe to remove without user approval.
Do not treat JSON-like JavaScript objects as valid strict JSON.

faq.question: Can this skill fix invalid JSON?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on common issues such as trailing commas, single quotes, comments, unquoted keys, and invalid values.

faq.question: Can it format minified JSON?
faq.answer: Yes. It can reformat minified JSON with consistent indentation and preserve the original data values.

faq.question: Does it validate against JSON Schema?
faq.answer: It can help with schema validation if you provide the schema and the JSON document to compare.

faq.question: Can it convert JSON to YAML?
faq.answer: Yes. It can convert JSON to YAML and preserve the structure, arrays, objects, and primitive value types.

faq.question: Will it execute JSON or run scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation-only guidance and does not include executable code or external command usage.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the full JSON text, the expected format, and whether changes like key sorting or comment removal are allowed.
